matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
seeded_markers: AKT1 E17K model (AKT1 activating pleckstrin-homology-domain model)
AKT1 pathway-activation model (AKT1 signaling activation model)
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a AKT1 AKT signaling thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to AKT kinase signaling, glucose and growth-factor response, apoptosis restraint, mTOR activation, and mosaic activation biology and PI3K-AKT-mTOR pathway biology, Somatic oncology and mosaic overgrowth context. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: GT-confirmed AKT1 E17K model dosage suggests this sample carries the AKT1 prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests a AKT1 AKT signaling thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to AKT kinase signaling, glucose and growth-factor response, apoptosis restraint, mTOR activation, and mosaic activation biology and PI3K-AKT-mTOR pathway biology, Somatic oncology and mosaic overgrowth context. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support. This specific marker is curated as AKT1 activating pleckstrin-homology-domain model. AKT1 E17K model is curated as a AKT1 marker for PI3K-AKT-mTOR pathway biology, Somatic oncology and mosaic overgrowth context. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the AKT kinase signaling, glucose and growth-factor response, apoptosis restraint, mTOR activation, and mosaic activation biology pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched AKT1 E17K model at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the AKT1 prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Can modify or tag AKT kinase signaling, glucose and growth-factor response, apoptosis restraint, mTOR activation, and mosaic activation biology.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a AKT1 AKT signaling thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to AKT kinase signaling, glucose and growth-factor response, apoptosis restraint, mTOR activation, and mosaic activation biology and PI3K-AKT-mTOR pathway biology, Somatic oncology and mosaic overgrowth context. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support. An observed AKT1 variant places this sample in the curated AKT1 research context. The local AKT1 bundle is AKT signaling oriented. Interpret markers through exact allele class, zygosity, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context rather than as stand-alone deterministic calls. AKT1 variants can alter or tag AKT kinase signaling, glucose and growth-factor response, apoptosis restraint, mTOR activation, and mosaic activation biology; effect direction depends on allele class, tissue, dosage, and biological context.

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a AKT1 variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests a AKT1 AKT signaling thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to AKT kinase signaling, glucose and growth-factor response, apoptosis restraint, mTOR activation, and mosaic activation biology and PI3K-AKT-mTOR pathway biology, Somatic oncology and mosaic overgrowth context. AKT1 methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a AKT kinase signaling, glucose and growth-factor response, apoptosis restraint, mTOR activation, and mosaic activation biology gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data. The local AKT1 bundle is AKT signaling oriented. AKT1 variants can alter or tag AKT kinase signaling, glucose and growth-factor response, apoptosis restraint, mTOR activation, and mosaic activation biology; effect direction depends on allele class, tissue, dosage, and biological context.
